title: Thousands rally in Manila May Day protests demanding wage increases and opposing US-Israeli military action
summary: Thousands of Filipino activists rallied in Manila as part of global May Day demonstrations, calling for higher wages and an end to US and Israeli military action in Iran, blending domestic labor grievances with international security concerns.
contents:
## Manila May Day Protests Target Wages and Foreign Military Actions

### Demonstration Overview
Thousands of activists took to the streets of Manila on May 1, 2026, joining global May Day demonstrations. The Philippine protests merged traditional labor demands with sharp criticism of international military operations.

### Demands and Grievances
Filipino protesters articulated a dual set of demands:
- **Domestic**: Higher minimum wages, improved labor protections, and action on rising cost of living
- **International**: An end to US and Israeli military action in Iran, reflecting growing concern about regional instability

### Context within Philippine Politics
The protests occur against a backdrop of complex Philippines security dynamics, including:
- Ongoing South China Sea territorial disputes with China
- Strengthened US-Philippines defense cooperation under the Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement (EDCA)
- Rising cost of living affecting Filipino workers
- Active labor movement with historical May Day significance in the Philippines

### Regional Significance
The Manila demonstrations formed part of a broader wave of Asian May Day protests, with parallel demonstrations occurring in Seoul, South Korea, and cities across Southeast Asia. The anti-military intervention messaging reflects growing regional sentiment against escalation in the Persian Gulf.
